mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate signal an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This raises the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns as valuations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC initiated rate hikes in March 2022 in response to high inflation.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a >0.20pp monthly FEDFUNDS increase during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate indicates a strong labor market and robust economic activity. This strength supports corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to positive equity returns. The signal is based on the level of unemployment.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in March 2022 is near pre-pandemic lows, indicating a tight labor market.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of UNRATE (inverted) and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When high consumer price inflation outpaces the growth in nominal retail sales, real consumer demand is contracting. This contraction reduces expected corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 3-month % change in CPIAUCSL > 3-month % change in RSAFS
evidence_refs: fact:CPI inflation is at multi-decade highs in early 2022.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when 3-month CPI growth exceeds 3-month RSAFS growth fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production reflects expanding economic output and corporate activity. This growth in real output is a fundamental driver of earnings and supports positive forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has shown resilience post-COVID shock.
failure_condition: The hit rate (correctly predicting the sign of 20-day returns) of a signal based on the sign of the monthly change in INDPRO is less than 52% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the year-over-year consumer price index forces the central bank to pursue restrictive monetary policy. The anticipation of future rate hikes leads to a contraction in equity multiples and negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2022 CPI YoY was 8.5%, a 40-year high.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 5%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than periods where it is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate and strong nonfarm payrolls growth signals an overheating labor market. This leads to wage-push inflation, compressing corporate profit margins, and forces hawkish central bank action, both of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 3.6% and recent PAYEMS prints have been strong, indicating an overheating labor market.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a positive average 60-day forward return during periods when both trigger conditions (UNRATE < 4.0%, PAYEMS avg > 200k) are met.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a contraction in a key cyclical sector of the economy. This indicates weakening consumer confidence and investment, portending a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is highly sensitive to interest rates, which are rising in 2022.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a month where HOUST falls by more than 5% is non-negative over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A higher-than-expected quarterly growth rate in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand and underlying economic momentum. This positive surprise revises near-term earnings expectations upward, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic health.
failure_condition: The correlation between the quarterly % change in GDPC1 and the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return (post-release) is not statistically positive (p > 0.05).
